title: Operation of the sigsuspend Function
description: A flowchart depicting the sequence of steps performed by a process using the sigsuspend function to atomically unblock signals and wait for a specific signal.
elements: Process with a signal mask blocking SIGUSR1
Process is suspended, waiting for any signal
sigsuspend returns -1 with errno set to EINTR
relationships: Process calls sigsuspend to change its mask and suspend
sigsuspend atomically swaps the process's signal mask, making it empty
The process is now able to receive any signal and enters a suspended state
The arrival of SIGUSR1 interrupts the suspension
The registered signal handler for SIGUSR1 runs
After the handler completes, the process resumes from the sigsuspend call
sigsuspend restores the original signal mask that was in place before the call
The function returns, indicating it was interrupted by a signal
